title: Senior Software Developer - Olink NPX
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.

**Location/Division Specific Information:**


We are the Proteomic Sciences division (former Olink Proteomics). Our solutions are transforming the understanding of human diseases through advanced protein biomarker discovery. With our innovative Olink solutions – including highly specific and validated assays that cover more than 5,000 proteins – our colleagues and technology empower scientists in groundbreaking research that will play a pivotal role in the future of personalized medicine.

**Position Description:**


We are looking for a Senior Software Developer to join our Olink NPX Map team as a part of our Software Development department at our HQ in Uppsala. Our Software Developers work in agile ways and across the stack to build innovative, ground-breaking applications.


NPX Map is a data analysis platform, handling pipelines that supports our Olink® Reveal, Olink® Explore HT and Olink® Explore 3072 products. It’s used by our customers to generate and quality control the proteomics data that comes out of the laboratories and is an integral part of groundbreaking medical studies

**Main Responsibilities**

* Develop and maintain NPX Map in a high quality manner, whilst working together with fellow developers and data scientists.
* Take architectural and technical decisions in collaboration with your teammates.
* Contribute to prioritization and planning of work according to business value.
* Participate in agile ceremonies and contribute to continuous improvement of the team's processes.

**Qualifications**

* Minimum of 5+ years of proven industry experience in software development
* Strong knowledge of C#/.NET as well as some kind of frontend framework (we use Avalonia UI)
* Well-versed in modern development tools
* Openness to work across the stack
* Full professional proficiency in English and Swedish is a must
* Knowledge of biological sciences and bioinformatics is a strong plus


If you are a curious person, that likes and excel in learning new things in a collaborative and open setting, this might just be the right position for you.


You should be based around the Uppsala or Stockholm region and able to attend our Uppsala HQ on a regular basis, we work 3 days from the office with flexibility of 2 days from home.


**Apply today!**


If you believe you’re the right fit for this role, we encourage you to submit your resume along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience.


Thermo Fisher Scientific is an EEO/Affirmative Action Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: Front-End Developer, Payment Experience
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  





 About The Role
 




 We are looking for an all-star Front-End Developer with strong Typescript experience to join our team in Stockholm, Sweden. You’ll primarily be working on improving the Checkout experience for millions of customers across Europe. You are a frontend developer by heart, who is eager to take responsibility and drive things end-to-end.
 




  





 About The Payment Experience Team
 




 The Payment Experience team manages the payin and deposit product at Trustly, which is one of our core offerings. The team is a mix of frontend and backend engineers, working closely with UX and neighbouring teams. On a daily basis, the team focuses on improving existing product flows and managing new or changing integrations, with the aim to create the highest converting payment product on the market.
 





  




**What you'll do** 
* Be a part of our payment team and build the UI for the next-generation payment platform using
 **React** 
 .
* Learn new methods and techniques in a team where
 **pair and mob programming** 
 is part of the daily methodology.
* Work closely with other developers, UX Designers, and product owners on a daily basis.
* Maintain a high focus on writing clean, testable code.
* Analyze large volumes of data to find possible problems and propose performance improvements.
* Identify performance issues and propose solutions.
* Support new developers on the company and train them on the development process, patterns, and features.
* Participate in functional meetings with non-engineering teams, offering knowledge of all front-end features and skills to propose solutions.
* Be able to participate in incident meetings and create an action plan.
* Drive technical initiatives from ideation to completion, influencing how your team collaborates and ships code.
* Design, develop, test, deploy, maintain, and improve software; take full responsibility for the lifecycle of your code



  




**Who you are** 
* Expert understanding and deep practical knowledge of state management, API integration, and frontend testing frameworks, ensuring robust and maintainable code
* Highly skilled in JavaScript/TypeScript, including ES6+ features, asynchronous operations, and contemporary web development methodologies
* Significant practical experience with current web frameworks such as React
* Demonstrated success in building fast, high-performance web applications, with a focus on speed optimisation, adherence to accessibility standards (WCAG), and reliable cross-browser functionality
* Familiarity with CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery) practices
* Familiar with using AI-tools in software development
* Ability to navigate across the entire technology stack, not limited to the frontend scope
* Strong communication skills and fluency in English


 Also nice if you have
 * Experience in mobile app development, specifically covering both iOS and Android platforms
* Knowledge of infrastructure orchestration using Kubernetes and Istio for traffic flow management
* Proficient in application monitoring, leveraging tools like Datadog for system reliability
* Experience of Java for building scalable and maintainable backend services
* Expertise in end-to-end testing methodologies, utilising modern frameworks like Playwright
* Familiarity with deployment control, specifically using feature flag tools like Config Cat
* Interest in User Experience (UX), ensuring technical deliverables align with design principles
* Proven ability to manage merchant integrations and provide direct technical customer support
* 
* Responsible and proactive in driving things end-to-end and a willingness to act as a cross-team bridge between different teams and systems
* A collaborative spirit, as you will be one of a small number of Front-end developers—it is important that we do not work in a silo






  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Full Stack Engineer
description:
**About Meela** 

 At Meela, we’re on a mission to make it easier to find the right mental health support, starting with matching people to the therapist that’s the best fit for them. Since launching, we’ve helped over 100,000 people across Sweden and the UK, and we’re just getting started. We’re backed by leading VCs and were recently named one of Europe’s hottest startups.
 



  





 Our founder Tiffany was named Female Founder of the Year in 2024. But what we’re most proud of is the feedback we get from users: “I finally found someone who gets me.”
   




**About the role** 

 We are looking for a passionate Full Stack Developer to join our team of 3 to help build the next stages of the Meela platform. You'll be working on features that directly affect how users find and connect with their ideal therapist - from onboarding flows to therapist matching logic and ongoing communication tools. Your work will directly shape the user experience for both clients and therapists.
 



 This is a hybrid role that is based out of our Gothenburg or Stockholm office.
   




**What you’ll be doing** 
* Building modern, user-friendly frontend features with TypeScript and Solid.js
* Developing robust backend services in Rust and shaping how our systems evolve
* Designing and maintaining APIs that power our platform
* Collaborating closely with product and design to deliver experiences users love
* Contributing to technical decisions that guide our overall architecture (we welcome all idea and voices!)
* Troubleshooting issues, fixing bugs, and optimising performance when needed
* Stepping into different parts of the stack when required - startup life keeps things varied!


**What we're looking for** 
* An experienced full-stack developer with a genuine interest in mental health and building tech that makes a difference
* Comfortable (and happy) working both backend and frontend
* Good understanding of relational databases and SQL (we use Postgres)
* Strong background in TypeScript, ideally with some exposure to frameworks like Solid.js
* Interest in learning and growing with Rust (prior experience a bonus, not essential)
* Someone who thrives in a fast-moving, iterative environment
* A problem-solver who can work independently but enjoys collaborating with others
* Clear communicator who enjoys collaboration and shared ownership
* Motivated by impact and purpose – not just code


**Hiring process** 

 1. First chat with CTO
 



 2. Small coding task
 



 3. Technical interview with development team and CTO
 



 4. Final chat with CEO
 



 We move fast and give feedback between steps.
   




**Residency requirements** 

 Applicants must be based in Sweden and have the legal right to work here. 
 


**If you’re excited about making a real impact in mental health and ready to grow with us, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now and join the ride!**

title: System Developer
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone’s contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimize our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As a System Developer in our Stockholm headquarters, you’ll join a skilled development team working at the intersection of fashion, retail, and technology. This role is ideal for someone with 1-2 years of experience in Dynamics 365 F&O and Azure integrations who is ready to take the next step and deepen their expertise in a dynamic, global environment.
   

  

 You’ll collaborate closely with senior developers, testers, IT Business Partners, and teams across the company. Together, you’ll design, build, and maintain solutions that support our global operations - from purchasing and logistics to retail and finance. Our tech stack includes Dynamics 365 F&O, Azure integration services, and Azure DevOps.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

 WHAT YOU WILL DO
   

  

 As System Developer your main contribution will be to build and maintain solutions that support our global operations. You will report to the Head of System Development & Business Relations. You will play a vital role in the development team where you will:
   

  

* Develop, test, deploy, and maintain software solutions and integrations that align with our architectural and coding standards
* Grow your craft with coaching from experienced senior developers
* Contribute to solution design and take an active role in technical discussions
* Write clean, structured, and well‑documented code
* Participate in code reviews and offer constructive, thoughtful feedback
* Troubleshoot and resolve defects, performance challenges, and technical debt
* Partner with IT Business Partners to assess needs and estimate development efforts
* Provide third‑line support and help diagnose production issues
* Collaborate closely with QA to refine testing practices and shape effective test strategies
* Highlight risks, gaps, and potential business impact throughout development
* Continuously seek ways to improve our tools, processes, and systems
* Share knowledge generously and contribute to a collaborative, learning‑focused culture


 WHO YOU ARE
   

  

 You have high aspirations and focus on details that create substantial impact. You enjoy working as a team and you embrace feedback to develop yourself and others. You are curious and thrive in a dynamic environment. Cooperation, respect, and clear communication are important, along with your ambition to learn and grow. If these qualities speak to you, we would love to have you on our team.
   

  

 Specific personal qualities for this role:
   

  

* You enjoy solving problems and understanding how systems support business processes
* You’re driven by improving how things work and creating clarity in complexity
* You feel comfortable asking questions, sharing ideas, and collaborating closely with others
* You’re eager to grow your skills - and you value a workplace where clarity, simplicity, and quality truly matter


 Specific experience and knowledge for this role:
   

  

* 1-2 years of hands‑on experience with Dynamics 365 F&O development (X++)
* Experience with Azure integrations is a strong plus
* A solid understanding of software development principles and coding standards
* Experience working in - or supporting - processes within retail is an advantage
* A structured, solution‑oriented approach to work
* Curiosity and an interest in AI, automation, and innovation
* A collaborative mindset and a genuine interest in learning and contributing
* Fluency in English


 What matters most to us is not whether you master all the listed knowledge and skills. We are dedicated to recognising the potential in each individual. We embrace diversity of people as well as ideas and viewpoints and encourage everyone's individual expression of their own identity.

title: Staff Software Engineer, Core Engine
company: Unity South APAC (SEA, ANZ, IND Subcont.)
description:
The opportunity
   

  

 We're looking for a Staff Engineer to join the Runtime team within the Core Engine group here at Unity. You'll be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining the low-level systems that form the backbone of the Unity runtime. You'll work across subsystems including transforms, the player loop, the job system, serialization, and support higher level features like physics and netcode. Your work will directly impact the performance, stability, and capabilities available to millions of developers worldwide.
   

  

 This is a highly technical individual contributor role with significant influence over architectural decisions and engineering practices. You'll collaborate closely with teams across rendering, physics, animation, scripting, and platform engineering to ensure the core engine meets the evolving needs of real-time 3D content creation.
   

  

 This role requires deep expertise in C++ and systems programming, a track record of technical leadership, and the ability to mentor engineers while driving critical initiatives that shape how creators build with Unity.
   

  

 What you'll be doing
   

  

* Design and implement core runtime systems with a focus on performance, memory efficiency, and cross-platform compatibility
* Lead technical initiatives spanning multiple teams, driving alignment on architecture and implementation approaches
* Profile, analyze, and optimize engine performance across diverse hardware targets including consoles, mobile devices, desktop, and emerging platforms
* Mentor and support engineers across the organization, raising the bar for code quality and engineering rigor
* Contribute to technical strategy and roadmap planning for the core engine
* Write clear technical documentation and participate in design reviews
* Debug complex, system-level issues that span multiple subsystems
* Evaluate and integrate new platform capabilities and hardware features


 What we're looking for
   

  

* Extensive experience in systems programming, with deep expertise in C++
* Proven experience building and shipping low-level engine or runtime systems at scale
* Strong understanding of computer architecture, memory hierarchies, cache behavior, and CPU/GPU interaction
* Experience with multithreaded programming
* Familiarity with multiple platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux, consoles, mobile) and their toolchains
* Track record of leading cross-team technical initiatives and influencing engineering direction
* Excellent debugging and profiling skills
* Strong written and verbal communication skills


 You might also have
   

  

* Experience with game engine development (Unity, Unreal, proprietary engines)
* Experience with C# and the managed runtime
* Familiarity with SIMD programming
* Experience with Burst, LLVM, or other compiler technologies
* Contributions to open-source projects or published technical writing


 Additional information
   

  

* Relocation support is not available for this position


**Benefits**
 At Unity, we want our team members to thrive. We offer a wide range of benefits designed to support well-being and work-life balance.
   

  

 Please note: Benefits eligibility, specific offerings, and coverage vary based on the country and employment status.
   

  

 While specific benefits vary, here are some of the ways we strive to take care of our eligible team members globally: Comprehensive health, life, and disability insurance | Commute subsidy | Employee stock ownership | Competitive retirement/pension plans | Generous vacation and personal days | Support for new parents through leave and family-care programs | Office food snacks | Mental Health and Wellbeing programs and support | Employee Resource Groups | Global Employee Assistance Program | Training and development programs | Volunteering and donation matching program
   

  

 Life at Unity
   

  

 Unity [NYSE: U] is the world’s leading game engine, powering play for more than 3 billion consumers each month. The top mobile games in the world, the most played PC indie titles, the most innovative console games, and virtually all of the top XR and Web Games are developed, deployed, and grown in Unity. Unity also enables teams across industries like automotive, manufacturing, and healthcare to design, simulate, and collaborate in 3D — closing the gap between ideas and reality. For more information, please visit www.unity.com.
   

  

*Unity is a proud equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive, innovative environment and celebrate our employees across age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law. Our differences are strengths that enable us to support the growing and evolving needs of our customers, partners, and collaborators.*

title: Software Developer - DevOps & Cloud
description:
Nasdaq is the world's largest provider of Market Technology, delivering top-of-the-line trading, clearing, and settlement technology and advisory services to exchanges and partners around the world.
   

  

 Are youready for a dynamic environment where you can contribute to the development of products with global impact?
   

  

 Eqlipse CSD is looking for a
 **Software Developer - DevOps and Cloud** 
 interested in joining the Eqlipse CSD product engineering team. Our CSD product helps markets/countries to meet the ever-increasing demands of the post-trade value chain with local & international settlement, corporate actions, custody & registrar services as well as financial services such as pledges, SBLs etc. Innovation and efficiency are two of our core values, and we search for candidates who share those values along with a genuine desire and drive to deliver top technological solutions to today’s markets.
   

  

**With this position we offer:**
 An exciting opportunity to work within a highly skilled and experienced product engineering team. You will belong to the
 **cloud & platform team Nexus.** 
 It´s a scrum team consisting of other DevOps and Cloud experts. Your focus will be on helping design and build the entire Eqlipse CSD Platform on Cloud, along with a heavy "hyper-automation" mindset on DevOps. A few of the technologies involved: AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes, Helm, Kafka, Gitlab, Java.
   

  

**What you will be doing:**
* Develop our Provisioning, Build and Deployment pipelines
* Create and setup existing and new Cloud accounts
* Create and setup Dev/QA/Performance/UAT environments for different clients
* Create comprehensive documentation and guides on how to build, deploy and run our systems
* Optimize our Cloud footprint for lower costs, and higher performance
* Automate manual processes and help our teams to have a better Developer Experience and higher efficiency and productivity
* Be our Kafka expert and mentoring other engineers to build their own Kafka knowledge
* Contribute with your experience by mentoring other engineers and being active in peer reviews, sprint planning, retrospectives, regression testing and demos


**What we are looking for:**
* Great attitude!
* Passionate about Cloud, DevOps, Infrastructure and Automation
* Deep Kafka knowledge
* Excellent communication and working proficiency in English
* Willingness to share knowledge and help team to grow in seniority
* People who enjoy working in a collaborative, agile and dynamic environment


**It would be great if you have:**
* A genuine interest to work in the FinTech industry and learn the domain, specifically within the post trade area, CSD or clearing
* Experience with Cloud/AWS/Docker/Kubernetes/Terraform/CICD
* Experience with Java and Distributed Systems
* Kafka knowledge (Strimzi/Managed Kafka)
* Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or equivalent experience


**Does this sound like you?**
 We value our talented employees, and whenever possible strive to help one of our associates grow professionally before recruiting new talent to our open positions. If you think the open position, you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply!
   

  

 This is a full-time permanent position located in Stockholm, Sweden. Nasdaq offers you a hybrid workplace to find a balance between working from home and collaborating in the office, therefore it's required to come into the office at least 3 days per week.
   

  

 Desired start date is at you earliest convenience. As the selection and interview process is ongoing, please submit your application in English as soon as possible.
   

  

**Come as You Are**
 Nasdaq is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, color, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law.
   

  

 We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request an accommodation.

title: Senior Software Development Engineer in Test (Media)
description:
**About us @Symphony**
 We’ve spent the last 10 years building a communication and markets technology company, powered by interconnected platforms: messaging, voice, directory and analytics. Over 1000 institutions use our modular technology built for global finance. Security is in our DNA with uncompromising data protection, end-to-end encryption and resilient architecture, all created on a foundation of trust with our customers .
   

  

**But that was only chapter one.** 
 We’re now building on our purpose-built network, expanding AI-powered, real-time collaboration, redefining flexibility with fully cloud-native software with our trader voice product, and rethinking the industry’s approach to identity verification, connection and intelligence. .
   

  

**The opportunity and our ambition are huge** 
 . But we need passionate, dedicated individuals to get there. At Symphony we work hard and fast. Our unique blend of technology and financial services makes it an environment you won't get elsewhere.
   

  

**Role Description**
 We are looking for a highly skilled and motivated Senior SDET (Software Development Engineer in Test), Media to take a lead role in ensuring the quality and reliability of our media backend and frontend components. In this role, you will design, develop, and maintain test automation frameworks, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and drive a shift-left testing mindset across the organization.
   

  

 As a technical leader, you will be responsible for supporting the execution of the Media Test Strategy, ensuring efficient test coverage, and contributing to the delivery of high-quality media features. Your expertise in media technologies (WebRTC, SIP), strong programming background, and ability to coordinate across teams will make you a key player in building scalable and reliable media solutions.
   

  

 If you are passionate about media technologies, automation, and quality leadership, we would love to hear from you!
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Take ownership of the quality strategy for media backend and frontend components.
* Design, implement, and maintain robust and scalable test automation frameworks.
* Collaborate closely with developers, product managers, and QA engineers to drive shift-left testing practices.
* Support and improve automated testing across different environments, including VDI, Cloud (GCP/AWS), and Windows 10/11 setups.
* Validate and troubleshoot real-time communication systems (WebRTC, SIP).
* Perform code reviews, mentor other SDETs, and ensure adherence to testing best practices.
* Coordinate with multiple teams and products to ensure seamless integration and quality delivery.
* Contribute to continuous improvement of test processes, tools, and methodologies.
* Support the execution and evolution of the Media Test Strategy.


**Required Qualifications** 
  

  

* Technical Skills
+ Strong programming skills in at least one language: Java, C#, C++, or TypeScript (C# is preferable).
+ Proven experience in designing and maintaining test automation frameworks.
+ Hands-on experience with media technologies (WebRTC, SIP).
+ Familiarity with cloud environments (GCP/AWS) and VDI setups.
+ Experience with Windows 10/11 environments.
+ Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines, version control (Git), and modern testing tools.
+ Strong understanding of software testing methodologies and shift-left practices.

* Soft Skills
+ Demonstrated leadership in driving quality initiatives across teams.
+ Excellent communication, collaboration, and coordination skills.
+ Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking abilities.
+ Ability to work in a fast-paced, cross-functional, and global environment.
+ Self-driven, proactive, and passionate about quality and automation.

* Education & Experience
+ Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience).
+ 5+ years of experience in software testing or SDET roles, with at least 2+ years in a senior/lead capacity.
+ Previous experience working in media-focused environments is highly desirable.


**Compensation**
* Competitive salary
* Bonus Plan
* Benefits and Perks vary based on location.


**Benefits And Perks**
* Regional specific competitive benefits
* Build your own Benefits (BYOB) perk
* Many other fun and exciting benefits and activities!


 We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment.

title: Guidewire Engineer
description:
Do you have a passion for simplifying the technical landscape, want to help us reshape the Mobility Insurance Industry, and understand the benefits of standard system implementations? We are now looking to expand our team with 3 additional Guidewire Engineers, join us!
   

  

**About The Team**
 As we continue modernizing our IT platforms, we are utilizing the power of standard customizable systems to support our core insurance business. This initiative is seen as a key success factor for If in the future to meet our business requirements and support our cooperation with the Mobility industry.
   

  

 We are in the delivering a new cloud-based core insurance system Guidewire InsuranceSuite for our partner offerings, need to mobilize the IT organization to make this transformation possible.
   

  

 We are an agile, innovative, and high-performing team dedicated to delivering high-quality software solutions in an iterative environment, and we look forward to having you on board.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Guidewire Engineer, you will have a key role in our dedicated IT unit, and are together with your team responsible for the implementation of our new cloud-based core insurance system. You will design, develop and deploy robust solutions using effective and modern engineering and agile practices, and continuously evolve them over time. It is important that you can navigate easily between various parts of the system and discuss technical solutions with cross functional teams also outside of the Guidewire context.
   

  

 We take modern development practices very seriously and expect an agile mindset with automated processes and quality by design.
   

  

**We offer**
 In the same way that we place high demands on you as an employee, we also expect you to place high demands on us as an employer. Here are some of the benefits of working at If:
   

  

* An including work environment where everyone is welcome
* Career and development opportunities in the largest insurance company in the Nordics
* Social activities, as well as highly skilled professional environment
* Possibility of hybrid workplace
* Health promoting workplace with e.g., wellness allowance and various sports activities
* Great insurance benefits
* Lunch bonus
* High pension savings


**Who You Are**
 We are looking for a driven professional with a passion for technology. You are solution-oriented, proactive, and enjoy working in a team environment. Your analytical mindset, coupled with your ability to communicate complex technical matters in a clear and uncomplicated way, makes you stand out. The working language is English, but knowing Swedish is also appreciated.
   

  

**You also have**
* A software engineering degree.
* Specialist or Ace certification as a Guidewire Developer in Policy Center or Billing Center.
* Specialist or Ace certification in Guidewire InsuranceSuite Integration.
* At least 2 years of proven experience in Guidewire InsuranceSuite Cloud implementation projects.
* Proficiency in Java, JSON and REST with Java development experience.
* Solid experience and interest in test automation principles and methodologies.
* Good understanding of Agile methodologies and modern Software Development Life Cycle.
* Experience with DevOps principles and technologies, emphasizing Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD).
* Excellent communication skills in English (Swedish is a bonus).
* Experience of the Swedish motor insurance market is meriting.


**Our promise to you**
 You will be part of an international, modern, and dynamic working environment within the leading insurance company in the Nordic region. You will have challenging and exciting tasks driving your personal and skills development. Training opportunities within and outside the organization will support your career growth. You will be at the forefront of delivering solutions aimed at the changing mobility landscape.
   

  

**Additional Facts And The Recruitment Process**
**Application deadline** 
 : Screening and interviewing will start immediately, and recruitment will be done continuously.
   

**To apply for the position** 
 : Please attach your CV
   

**Work location** 
 : Sweden (Bergshamra) (Swedish citizen or permanent residence permit)
   

  

**Start:** 
 As soon as possible or by agreement
   

  

 For more information, please contact Peter Hartlén, Sourcing Manager, peter.hartlen@if.se.
   

  

 A background check will be conducted on all final candidates.
   

  

 Read more about our Tech Community at If and how we together create tech that matters: http://www.if.se/tech
   

  

 Ready to be part of our dynamic team and drive impactful change? Apply today and seize this opportunity!

title: AI Product Manager
description:
*Vill du leda AI-initiativ som gör verklig skillnad i Sveriges största streaming- och medieorganisation?*
 Hos TV4 möts teknik, innovation, nyhetsjournalistik, sport och underhållning. Vi söker nu en AI Product Manager till vårt centrala AI Development Hub-team. Här får du möjligheten att driva prioritering av AI-initiativ, skapa arbetssätt och struktur, och hjälpa organisationen att utveckla skalbara AI-lösningar.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som AI Product Manager blir du navet för AI-frågor på TV4 och blir en del av Product Competence-teamet. Du leder AI utvecklingen på TV4 samt ett mindre utvecklingsteam team och ansvarar för att rätt initiativ prioriteras, följer upp på KPI:er och tillsammans med teamet säkerställer ni att AI-lösningarna ger värde för verksamheten. Du arbetar nära kollegor i hela organisationen och samarbetar även med Schibsted för att hitta synergier och skalbara lösningar nordiskt. I rollen har du också ett tydligt mandat och stora möjligheter att forma hur AI Dev Hubben arbetar, både vad gäller arbetssätt, strukturer och prioriteringsprocesser.
   

  

**Du kommer att:**
* Driva AI‑mognad i organisationen genom utbildningar, inspiration, och best practice‑delning.
* Formulera en långsiktig AI‑vision och roadmap som är förankrad i affärsstrategin.
* Identifiera och prioritera AI-initiativ som skapar värde för verksamheten.
* Leda arbetet med att sätta strukturer, arbetssätt och KPI:er för AI Dev Hub-teamet.
* Leda AI Advisory Board tillsammans med Legal.
* Fungera som brygga mellan affär, teknik, juridik och ledning.
* Hjälpa organisationen att implementera AI-lösningar.
* Säkerställa att initiativ kan skalas och integreras med befintliga verktyg och plattformar.
* Nordiskt ansvar att driva AI arbetet inom TV4 och MTV.


 Vi ser att du har god teknisk förståelse och erfarenhet av AI-management eller produktledning. Rollen är strategiskt viktig och kräver förmåga att se helheten, prioritera, och driva AI-frågor på ett pragmatiskt och värdedrivet sätt.
   

  

**Vem du är**
 För att lyckas i rollen tror vi att du har erfarenhet av att arbeta med produkter och initiativ där AI används för att skapa värde och effektivisera verksamheten. Du har flera års erfarenhet av produktutveckling inom tech- eller dataintensiva miljöer och har som Product Manager lett tvärfunktionella team bestående av utvecklare och jobbat nära stakeholders från olika delar av organisationen.
   

  

 Du är nyfiken och driven att förstå verksamhetens behov och möjliga AI-lösningar. Du har förmågan att se helheten och förstår hur AI-lösningar, teknik och affär samspelar. Du är proaktiv och prestigelös, tar initiativ och får saker att hända. Du motiveras av att arbeta i en organisation som befinner sig på en transformationsresa, där du får vara med och forma hur AI och produktarbete utvecklas Nordiskt på TV4.
   

  

**Dina styrkor och erfarenheter:**
* Erfarenhet av AI-management, data science, eller AI-plattformsutveckling.
* Erfarenhet av att prioritera och driva AI-initiativ i en organisation med många idéer och behov.
* Erfarenhet av användarcentrerad produktutveckling eller att implementera AI-lösningar i verksamheter.
* Datadriven och analytisk, med fokus på KPI:er och tydliga resultat.
* Trygg teknisk förståelse och förmåga att agera bollplank i komplexa beslut kring AI och data.
* Kan kombinera strategiskt tänkande med hands-on närvaro i det dagliga arbetet.


**Det är ett plus om du har:**
* Teknisk bakgrund, t.ex. ingenjörsutbildning, eller erfarenhet av att arbeta nära utvecklingsteam inom AI eller data.
* Vana vid cyklisk produktutveckling och moderna agila arbetssätt.


**Detta får du hos TV4**
 Tjänsten är placerad i Stockholm (TV4-huset) med gångavstånd till Gärdets tunnelbana. Kontoret erbjuder eget gym, parkeringsgarage, restaurang samt takterrass. Rollen erbjuder även:
   

  

* Kollektivavtal, med tjänstepension, friskvårdsbidrag m.m
* Vara en del av TV4s ekosystem och ta del av de tjänster som TV4 erbjuder sina kunder.
* Rabatter via Benify.
* En genuin kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling där du även har chans att växa inom vår organisation.
* Arbete i team där vi tillsammans motiverar och driver TV4: till Sveriges bästa mediaplattform.


**Om TV4**
 TV4 har samlat Sverige i över 30 år, dygnet runt, varje dag. Miljontals besöker TV4 Play och våra sajter för att ta del av innehåll, när och var de vill. Din utveckling är viktig, både för dig och för TV4:s fortsatta resa.
   

  

**Praktisk information**
* Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning med 6 månaders provanställning.
* Facklig tillhörighet: Unionen eller SJF. Kontakta unionen@tv4.se eller sjf@tv4.se för frågor.


**Ansökan & process**
 Vi tillämpar löpande urval och intervjuer – så skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt genom ansök knappen! Den här tjänsten tillsätts i samarbete med Safemind.

title: Product Manager
description:
Over the past eight years we have provided smart tools that help put financial power back in the hands of the people – where we believe it belongs. Everything we do is based on one vision: to improve the financial health of our users - one credit at the time.
   

  

 So far, we’ve helped millions of users by refinancing expensive consumer loans. But we aim higher; new markets, new products and new ways of improving financial lives in an innovative way. As a Product Manager you will be responsible for our core offering joining a diverse scaleup environment with co-workers who are motivated, adaptable and committed to creating fantastic (we mean amazing) products.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
 We believe that everyone should get the interest rate they deserve, meaning that assisting our users with debt management will always be at the core of what we do. You’ll play a key role in shaping our core product (refinancing) while helping to explore and build new fintech solutions that people truly need. Together with a team of engineers, designers and other specialists, you’ll drive projects forward with focus, speed and a clear sense of ownership.
   

  

**Your Work Will Cover Things Like**
* Turning user needs into product opportunities and features.
* Supporting strategy by connecting product outcomes to business goals.
* Using data and insights to make smart decisions and run experiments.
* Coordinating with your team to keep roadmaps realistic, transparent and on track.
* Communicating progress and learnings across the company.


**Who You Are**
 We’re looking for someone who can make complex things simple, and who enjoys working hands-on in a fast-moving environment. You don’t need to have led large product organizations – what matters is that you’ve worked closely with engineers and designers and know how to bring an idea from concept to launch.
   

  

**We Believe You’ll Bring**
* Around 3+ years of experience in product management (or equivalent).
* Experience in building and improving digital products (web and/or app).
* Knowledge of product methodologies, user research and roadmapping.
* A strong sense of urgency and the ability to prioritize what really matters.
* A curious, commercial mindset and interest in what drives customer engagement.


 Fintech experience is a plus but not a must – we’ll help you get up to speed. What counts most is your drive to learn, execute and grow with us.
   

  

 In short we are looking for a pragmatic product generalist with a mix of business development and doer mentality. If you are still reading this we hope that sounds like a good fit? So let's get to the good stuff…
   

  

**We offer you**
 The chance to be part of something truly amazing. We aspire for a level of ambition that matches the scale of our mission. If you're ready to be a part of this transformation we want to hear from you. If you have high grit, an unwavering sense of urgency, and an unyielding commitment to excellence, we want you to be the driving force behind reshaping the financial world. This isn't just a job; it's a mission.
   

  

 We promise that you will be challenged, pushed to develop your skill set and get the opportunity to work alongside amazing and smart colleagues. At Anyfin, we hire great people and create the conditions for them to have real autonomy and impact, especially in product management. While we've had a good run so far it is what lies ahead that excites everyone that works here, and we are just getting started. Then there is the compensation package, centrally located cozy office, extra time off on your birthday and a bunch of other benefits as well. But we will get to all that in due time.
   

  

 For now begin your journey with us by clicking "Apply" below.
   

  

 We cannot wait to meet you!
   

  

*PS Please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role.*
 Anyfin is a fintech on a mission to simplify loan repayments and improve the financial well-being of millions. We help consumers reduce the costs of high-interest loans, and take control of their repayment plan.

title: Front-End Developer, Payment Experience
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  





 About The Role
 




 We are looking for an all-star Front-End Developer with strong Typescript experience to join our team in Stockholm, Sweden. You’ll primarily be working on improving the Checkout experience for millions of customers across Europe. You are a frontend developer by heart, who is eager to take responsibility and drive things end-to-end.
 




  





 About The Payment Experience Team
 




 The Payment Experience team manages the payin and deposit product at Trustly, which is one of our core offerings. The team is a mix of frontend and backend engineers, working closely with UX and neighbouring teams. On a daily basis, the team focuses on improving existing product flows and managing new or changing integrations, with the aim to create the highest converting payment product on the market.
 





  




**What you'll do** 
* Be a part of our payment team and build the UI for the next-generation payment platform using
 **React** 
 .
* Learn new methods and techniques in a team where
 **pair and mob programming** 
 is part of the daily methodology.
* Work closely with other developers, UX Designers, and product owners on a daily basis.
* Maintain a high focus on writing clean, testable code.
* Analyze large volumes of data to find possible problems and propose performance improvements.
* Identify performance issues and propose solutions.
* Support new developers on the company and train them on the development process, patterns, and features.
* Participate in functional meetings with non-engineering teams, offering knowledge of all front-end features and skills to propose solutions.
* Be able to participate in incident meetings and create an action plan.
* Drive technical initiatives from ideation to completion, influencing how your team collaborates and ships code.
* Design, develop, test, deploy, maintain, and improve software; take full responsibility for the lifecycle of your code



  




**Who you are** 
* Expert understanding and deep practical knowledge of state management, API integration, and frontend testing frameworks, ensuring robust and maintainable code
* Highly skilled in JavaScript/TypeScript, including ES6+ features, asynchronous operations, and contemporary web development methodologies
* Significant practical experience with current web frameworks such as React
* Demonstrated success in building fast, high-performance web applications, with a focus on speed optimisation, adherence to accessibility standards (WCAG), and reliable cross-browser functionality
* Familiarity with CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery) practices
* Familiar with using AI-tools in software development
* Ability to navigate across the entire technology stack, not limited to the frontend scope
* Strong communication skills and fluency in English


 Also nice if you have
 * Experience in mobile app development, specifically covering both iOS and Android platforms
* Knowledge of infrastructure orchestration using Kubernetes and Istio for traffic flow management
* Proficient in application monitoring, leveraging tools like Datadog for system reliability
* Experience of Java for building scalable and maintainable backend services
* Expertise in end-to-end testing methodologies, utilising modern frameworks like Playwright
* Familiarity with deployment control, specifically using feature flag tools like Config Cat
* Interest in User Experience (UX), ensuring technical deliverables align with design principles
* Proven ability to manage merchant integrations and provide direct technical customer support
* 
* Responsible and proactive in driving things end-to-end and a willingness to act as a cross-team bridge between different teams and systems
* A collaborative spirit, as you will be one of a small number of Front-end developers—it is important that we do not work in a silo






  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Technical Support Expert
description:
**This is Worldline** 




 Worldline helps businesses of all shapes and sizes to accelerate their growth journey - quickly, simply, and securely. We are the innovators at the heart of the payments technology industry, shaping how the world pays and gets paid. Our technology powers the growth of millions of businesses across 5 continents. And just as we help our customers accelerate their business, we are committed to helping our people accelerate their careers. Together, we shape the evolution.
 



  





**The Opportunity** 




 The Technical Support Team is one of the key functions within our organization. As a Technical Support Expert, you’ll be one of the main, and most important, contact points for our merchants in need of support. You love to help people, communicate, analyze, and troubleshoot the payment product we offer.
 



  





 You’ll be supporting the clients in their questions regarding PaymentIQ service, which is a fast, dynamic, complex, and powerful platform, with a vast number of integrations towards systems, payment methods and special setups. The complex and customizable nature of the system makes it technically demanding yet rewarding to be supporting.
 



  





**Day-to-Day Responsibilities** 



* Monitor ticket queues to resolve issues, receive and reply to support requests through Helpdesk application and telephone.
* Provide timely response, meeting defined service level agreements and internal support KPI and ensuring resolution to all reported issues



  





**Who Are We Looking For** 




 People who can drive positive change, step up and show what’s next – people with passion, can-do attitude and a hunger to learn and grow. In practice this means:
 


* You are customer focused and service minded with a great interest and understanding in technology
* Solution-driven with a strong analytical ability
* Love to communicate
* Takes ownership and responsibility for tasks you are assigned
* Truly flexible; able to shift tasks and priorities in a big way at the drop of a hat
* Strong customer service skills
* Previous experience as a Technical Support Agent both onsite and/or remote
* Fluent in English, both written and spoken
* Interest in, or knowledge in programming (Java, SQL, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Eclipse or Git) is an advantage
* Previous experience of the payment industry is an advantage



  





**Perks & Benefits** 




 At Worldline, you’ll get the chance to be at the heart of the global payments technology industry and shape how the world pays and gets paid. On top of that, you will also:
 


* Health contribution
* Health check – Every 2:nd year
* Private Health Care Insurance (EuroAccident)
* Parental pay and 12 months pension during parental leave
* Opportunity to hybrid working according to company standard
* Benefit portal with many great offers, plus more.



  





**Shape the evolution** 




 We are pushing towards the next frontiers of payments technology, and we look for big thinkers to join our journey. People with passion, can-do attitude and a hunger to learn and grow. Here you’ll work with ambitious colleagues from around the world, take on unique challenges as a team, and make a real impact on the society. And with our empowering culture, strong technology and extensive training opportunities, we help you accelerate your career. Wherever you decide to go. Join our global team of over 18,000 innovators across 40+ countries and shape a tomorrow that is yours to own.
 



  





 Learn more about life at Worldline at jobs.worldline.com
 



  





 We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as an individual with a disability, or any applicable legally protected characteristics.

title: Junior Nätverkstekniker
description:
*Har du en kandidatexamen inom nätverksteknik med erfarenhet inom nätverk och vill fortsätta utvecklas i din karriär? Här får du chansen att jobba hos en kund med spetskompetens inom området – i centrala Stockholm. Vi söker dig med driv och en positiv inställning!*
 Vi söker nu en Junior Nätverkstekniker till ett konsultuppdrag hos en kund med hög teknisk kompetens där du blir en del av ett engagerat team i en prestigelös miljö där samarbete, kunskapsdelning och arbetsglädje värderas högt.
   

  

 I rollen arbetar du nära erfarna kollegor och andra team för att säkerställa stabila och välfungerande nätverkstjänster för företagets kunder. Rollen har ett tydligt fokus på support, drift och ärendehantering.
   

  

**Exempel på arbetsuppgifter:**
* Ärendehantering och support för nätverksrelaterade frågor
* Felsökning av nätverkstjänster i dialog med kunder
* Konfiguration av kundplacerad nätverksutrustning
* Arbete med nätverksutrustning i befintlig intern infrastruktur
* Medverkan vid migrering och förändringar av nätverkstjänster
* Samarbete med kunder, leverantörer och interna team


 Rollen är både teknisk och social och passar dig som trivs i en supportroll med många kontaktytor. Du är kommunikativ, lösningsorienterad och motiveras av att hjälpa kunder samtidigt som du utvecklar dina tekniska kunskaper inom nätverk.
   

  

**För den här rollen ser vi att du har:**
* En högskole- eller universitetsutbildning inom nätverk, eller motsvarande relevant arbetslivserfarenhet inom IT eller telekom, som du tydligt motiveras i din ansökan
* Arbetslivserfarenhet inom relevant område
* Ett stort driv av att arbeta med ständiga förbättringar
* Ett brinnande intresse för IT och nätverskteknik
* Förmåga att kommunicera obehindrat på både svenska och engelska, i såväl tal som skrift


**Som person tror vi att du är:**
* Lösningsorienterad - där du tar egna initiativ och kommer med lösningsförslag för att komma framåt i ditt arbete
* Samarbetsvillig och kommunikativ - där du är vass på att skapa och underhålla kundrelationer samtidigt som du samarbetar väl med kollegor i teamet
* Strukturerad - där du kan planera och lägga upp ditt arbete på ett noggrant och strukturerat sätt


**Start** 
 : Omgående start med hänsyn till uppsägningstid. Uppdraget är på heltid och är tillsvidare
   

  

**Placeringsort:** 
 Stockholm
   

  

**Ansökan:** 
 Intervjuer sker löpande och tillsättning sker vid rätt match
   

  

**Vad vi står för:**
 Vi på Camai erbjuder ett av marknadens branschledande anställningsvillkor med stor frihet som ledord. Vi drivs av att hitta det bästa uppdraget för våra medarbetare. Det roligaste som finns är att hitta en perfekt matchning mellan konsult och kund. Vi vet att alla har ett drömarbete, och vi är ett tight entreprenörsdrivet team som arbetar för att se till att du tycker att det är roligt att gå till jobbet, varje dag. Vi tror att kompetenta människor dras till varandra och vill jobba med likasinnade i spännande kunduppdrag.
   

  

 Vi har en av marknadens mest förmånliga ersättningsmodell anpassad för en modern konsultorganisation med mycket frihet. Vi har helt enkelt kul tillsammans!
   

  

**Varför ska du välja oss?**
 Med många års erfarenhet i konsultbranschen har vi valt våra tre värdeord med omsorg; kommunikation, ambition och balans. För att lyckas tror vi att våra medarbetare får vara med och forma sitt arbete efter prioriteringar och intresse i livet. Med din ambition och rätt verktyg från oss ser vi till att du utvecklas och samtidigt belönas på vägen.
   

  

*Vill du vara en del av vår fortsatta tillväxt?*

title: IT Systems Administrator
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**Our Core Tech Stack**
 You'll work hands-on with a modern, integrated technology ecosystem designed for security, productivity, and collaboration. Our identity management foundation centers on Okta for access control and Entra ID for hybrid directory services. Daily productivity flows through Google Workspace for collaboration and Slack for team communication, while Zoom handles video conferencing needs.
   

  

 IT operations are streamlined with Workwize for asset management, and we leverage AI assistance through ChatGPT and Claude to enhance workflow efficiency. This stack supports our fast-paced environment while maintaining enterprise-grade security and scalability as we continue to grow.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
* Identity & Access Management - Administer and evolve our Okta environment including SSO integrations (SAML 2.0 & OIDC), lifecycle management, MFA policies, and Okta Workflows. Manage Entra ID for hybrid identity scenarios and conditional access policies.
* User Provisioning & Deprovisioning - Implement and maintain SCIM-based automated provisioning across SaaS applications. Own the full employee lifecycle from onboarding to offboarding, ensuring timely access grants and revocations.
* Google Workspace Administration - Manage Google Workspace at scale, including user and group management, email routing and DNS records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), shared drives, security policies, and organizational unit configuration.
* SaaS Management - Administer and optimise Slack, Zoom, Workwize, and other business-critical SaaS tools. Manage licenses, configurations, and integrations to support company growth.
* Apple Device Management - Own the full device lifecycle for macOS and iOS using an MDM platform (e.g. Kandji, Jamf). Handle Apple Business Manager, zero-touch deployment, OS patching, and endpoint security.
* Automation & Integration - Build automations and integrations across platforms using APIs, scripting (Python, Bash), and workflow tools. Reduce manual toil and improve operational efficiency.
* Security & Compliance - Collaborate with the Security team to implement Zero Trust principles, enforce conditional access policies, and maintain compliance controls aligned with frameworks such as ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR.
* Office IT & Infrastructure - Manage on-site infrastructure including Wi-Fi networks, conference room AV, and hardware provisioning. Plan and execute IT setup for new office locations.
* Technical Support - Provide responsive, high-quality technical support and guidance to employees across all applications, devices, and services.


**Who You Are**
* 5+ years of experience in IT administration, systems operations, or a similar role in a fast-paced technology environment.
* Identity & SSO expertise - Deep hands-on experience with Okta (or comparable IdP), including SAML 2.0, OIDC, SCIM provisioning, lifecycle management, and MFA policies.
* Directory services - Familiarity with Entra ID (Azure AD) for hybrid identity, conditional access, and directory synchronisation.
* Google Workspace power user - Strong knowledge of admin console management, email authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), DNS configuration, and org-level security policies.
* Apple ecosystem - Deep expertise in macOS and iOS administration, Apple Business Manager, and MDM tools such as Kandji, Jamf, or Intune.
* Scripting & APIs - Hands-on experience writing automation with Python, Bash, or PowerShell and integrating systems via REST APIs.
* Networking fundamentals - Solid understanding of DNS, DHCP, TLS/SSL, email routing, and general network troubleshooting.
* Familiarity with compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR) and how IT operations support them.
* Strong problem-solving mindset with a focus on automation, efficiency, and user experience.
* Excellent communication skills and the ability to build trust as a reliable point of contact across the organisation.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Enterprise Account Manager

title: Data & Analytics Senior Management Consultants/Managers
description:
**Senior Consultants and Managers to join our Intelligent Industry Data & Analytics team** 




  





 At Capgemini Invent, we believe difference drives change. As inventive transformation consultants, we blend our strategic, creative and scientific capabilities, collaborating closely with clients to deliver cutting-edge solutions. Join us to drive transformation tailored to our client's challenges of today and tomorrow. Informed and validated by science and data. Superpowered by creativity and design. All underpinned by technology created with purpose.
 



  





**Your Role** 




 Being part of the Intelligent Industry Data & Analytics team and helping our clients form strategies needed to become more data driven and mastering AI & Data Governance and Management through strategies, exploration, experimentation and execution. In this role you will play a key role in:
 


* Helping our clients understand the impact of Data/AI on a strategic, tactical and operational level and pave the way towards reaching their strategic objectives
* Helping our clients utilize data and govern data assets to reach strategic objectives and increase business value
* Working with innovative topics at the interface between business, strategy, data and AI



  





**Your Profile** 




 We believe you have experience, or strong interest, in one or several of the following areas: Procurement / Supply chain / Manufacturing / Engineering / R&D in combination with:
 


* Experience in one or several of the following areas: Data & AI Strategy / Data & AI Governance / Data Visualization / Process Mining & Automation / Functional Data & Analytics / Data & AI Compliance / AI Adoption / AI use case development / AI scaling / Agentic AI
* 1-7 years’ work experience, ideally from management consulting or equivalent
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
* A master’s degree with a business, technical or information technology or data & analytics focus
* Excellent Swedish and English language skills and Swedish work permit required.



  





**What You’ll Love About Working Here** 




 At Capgemini Invent, you’ll be part of a global team shaping the future of Intelligent Industry through data and analytics. We offer:
 


* Impactful Work: Help organizations unlock the full potential of data and AI to drive operational excellence, sustainability, and strategic decision-making. You’ll enable clients to become truly data-driven by defining strategies, governance frameworks, and execution plans that transform vision into reality.
* Innovation at Scale: Work at the intersection of business, strategy, data, and AI, leveraging cutting-edge technologies and methodologies to create intelligent products, operations, and services.
* Career Growth: Access world-class training, mentorship, and opportunities to deepen your expertise in AI, Data Governance, and advanced analytics—accelerating your professional journey.
* Collaborative Culture: Join a diverse, inclusive team that values curiosity, creativity, and bold ideas. Together, we explore, experiment, and execute strategies that make a real difference.
* Purpose-Driven Projects: Help clients harness data and AI to achieve strategic objectives, increase business value, and build sustainable, customer-centric solutions that shape the future.



  





**Location:** 
 In line with our trust-based culture, Capgemini Invent offers a hybrid working model, and for this role you can be based in our attractively located Stockholm or Gothenburg office, ready to travel to our clients’ sites when needed.
 



  





**As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as required.** 




  





**Sounds like the next step in your career?** 
 We look forward to receiving your application and learning more about your experience and aspirations.
 



  





**About Capgemini** 




 Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 423,400 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55‑year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end‑to‑end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market‑leading capabilities in AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem. The Group reported 2025 global revenues of €22.5 billion.

title: Interim- Senior Operations Lead- Sweden
description:
**Interim – Senior Operations Lead**
**Contract: 3-6 months (interim / temp)**
**Location: Stockholm (Sweden)**
**About MVH Europe**
 Mars Veterinary Health (MVH) Europe is a family of leading veterinary care providers committed to creating a better world for pets. Our network includes trusted brands such as AniCura and Linnaeus, operating over 500 animal hospitals and clinics across 17 European countries. Together, our teams deliver high-quality, modern veterinary care for companion animals, ensuring peace of mind for pet owners through excellent access and patient safety.
   

  

 AniCura, our largest brand in Europe, provides care to over three million patients each year through 12,500 dedicated professionals. Linnaeus, operating primarily in the UK and Ireland, is home to over 6,000 Associates empowered to deliver exceptional care through a culture of collaboration, clinical excellence, and continuous development.
   

  

 At MVH Europe, we prioritize a culture of accountability, inclusion, and wellbeing. We believe our purpose to make A BETTER WORLD FOR PETS starts with building a better world for our people. We support our teams through strong governance, comprehensive wellbeing programs, and a shared passion for delivering exceptional outcomes in veterinary care
   

  

**The role**
 For our Central Operations team, we are urgently looking for a
 **Senior** 
**Operations Lead** 
 who can support 2 large Veterinary health practices across a region in North west Europe. There could be additionally scope throughout the region also whereby additional project support is required.
   

  

 Ideally you will be based near Stockholm, Sweden to support the Operating Unit VP for this region.
   

  

 This role will suit an experienced operator who may previously have worked as a large scale multi site operator in Operations, or had their own practice in either Veterinary health or health care environments.
   

  

 As a proven operator, you will be used to working at pace, in turn key projects and used to a quick impact type of role in projects where the experience can lend itself to understanding and reading situations quickly, being able to find appropriate solutions and move forward with actions.
   

  

 This will suit someone with an agile mindset, comfortable working at pace and able to galvanise a team, land planning and activity in line with immediate business needs but also be nimble to change direction quickly inline with an agile business, reacting to wider business changes .
   

  

 You will interface with Practice Managers to lead, engage and support fixing and implementation, seeking effective solutions and to create and embed sustainable change. Therefore you will possess a strong and confident style to make great decisions and execute.
   

  

 There are no direct reports in this role however you will partnership with an operational analyst who can side by side inform and support decision making. You will report into Area Operational Directors and have a dotted line into Area Operational Managers. You will work also closely with a number of senior stakeholders, across both local operations and into the newly formed Central Operations team.
   

  

 This is a short term project that will augment an existing team who can free up time whilst projects and plans are being addressed in more detail so it is very on the ground, with a roll up the sleeves approach to daily operations. This could be extended dependent upon contribution to the business and created opportunities.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Agile working with broad operational remit
* Project thinking, working at pace
* Plan and land activity with immediate business needs but also be nimble to change direction quickly inline with an agile business
* Provide clear, structured and credible leadership at all levels
* Develop others to be equipped with skills to deliver themselves
* Engage and direct teams, influence and steer


**Your profile**
* Proven multi site operational experience
* Fluent English is required alongside Swedish and another language would be advantageous for overall support in the region which could lead to other project support
* Able to operate within North West Europe, with a strong focus on Stockholm, Sweden
* Knowledge of the immediate market in Sweden will be key as well as an understanding of the European wider market
* Strong ability to drill down into data and quickly identify patterns and issues
* Highly agile, hands-on and comfortable working in an interim / fast-paced environment
* Experience in pet healthcare is a strong plus
+ Alternatively, experience in dental, healthcare or multi-site environments is highly relevant

* Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
* Credible and confident communicator to interface and escalate at senior business levels, produce information and presentations


**Why this role?**
* High-impact interim assignment with clear priorities
* Continued project exposure to multi site operations
* Opportunity to make a visible difference in a short time frame
* International scope within a Central Operations environment
